Bit only accepts unsigned integers (as the minus sign is an operator in reality and we do not process operators). The range is 0..&FFFFFFFF, the full longint range. This should be sufficient for most applications. Base can be from 2 to theoretically maxint, but the charset will limit this practically to 10 26 chars for coding a digit. So the biggest possible base would be 36. There is no range checking (Which should not be of any problem, provided I did program this correctly (it has been tested with range checking) and there is no overflow checking. Otherwise, if you want to get rid of the 'hand' cursor in your stack permanently without the necessity of an inefficient or impractical idle handler, then read on... The hand cursor in HyperCard actually lives in 2 resources. The first, and most difficult to alter, is the (until now) permanent default cursor for HyperCard. This cursor and it's mask are in the '_12' font in HyperCard. The other 'hand' cursor looks exactly the same; it's the one used when you give the command, 'set cursor to hand,' from a script. It's a real CURS resource. To permanently change the default cursor to something other than the hand, you will have to do three things: edit the CURS resource and edit the '_12' font, and, finally, you have to include the following command in your openStack handler to activate the new default cursor: 'choose browse tool'. This will insure that the real hand is purged for the new default cursor. This editing of the default cursor was done with HyperCard 1.2.2.
